report_name: ShinyHunters Ransomware Spree: Carnival, Zara's Parent, and 40+ Firms Breached in Massive Campaign

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A threat group, historically known for selling stolen databases, that has adopted a ransomware and double-extortion model. They claimed responsibility for a large-scale campaign compromising over 40 organizations worldwide, using the stolen data to extort victims.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of victim organizations through ransomware deployment and the threat of public data leaks.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors exfiltrated large volumes of sensitive data to attacker-controlled cloud storage services to blend in with normal traffic as part of their double-extortion tactics.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The core of the ransomware attack involved encrypting critical systems to disrupt victim operations and coerce ransom payments.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The campaign likely leveraged stolen credentials, a common tactic for groups like ShinyHunters, to gain initial access and move laterally within victim networks.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Sophisticated phishing campaigns are a likely initial access vector used by the attackers to compromise target organizations.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Legitimate tools like RDP are typically used by attackers in these campaigns for lateral movement across the network.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: The legitimate tool 'rclone.exe' is frequently abused by threat actors in similar campaigns to exfiltrate staged data to various cloud storage backends.

tools.name: 7-Zip
tools.description: The archiving utility '7z.exe' is commonly used by attackers to stage and compress sensitive data into password-protected archives before exfiltration.

identities.name: Carnival Corporation
identities.description: A high-profile victim of the ShinyHunters ransomware campaign. Its subsidiary, Holland America Line, had 8.7 million records exposed.

identities.name: Holland America Line
identities.description: A subsidiary of Carnival Corporation that was breached, resulting in the exposure of 8.7 million records.

identities.name: Mytheresa
identities.description: A global retail company listed as a victim on the ShinyHunters data leak site.

identities.name: Pitney Bowes
identities.description: A global technology company listed as a victim on the ShinyHunters data leak site.

identities.name: Inditex
identities.description: The parent company of Zara, listed as a victim in the widespread ShinyHunters ransomware campaign.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Data Exfiltration and Leak Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The campaign utilized attacker-controlled cloud storage or dedicated servers for data exfiltration and a public data leak site to pressure victims in their double-extortion scheme.

indicators.name: Anomalous Outbound Data Transfer to Cloud Storage
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Outbound Data Transfer to Cloud Storage
id: 2b1a9c8d-e3f4-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects potentially anomalous large outbound data transfers to common cloud storage providers.
  This is a hunting hint from a report on ShinyHunters, who use legitimate cloud services for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQHnoEMqX-ZN1SABXBdpLEM5_A_7FZu4FuyiiBsz3x2UtkfgzccY6psCNIcRrG28JQVPw9J8lXCWwH_WLR_0oJbt1osmUS1FELlDp5qqNpRxzYBtV-47pWzlO9EyEz4-d9wtupq6QfLmdxA=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith:
      - '.mega.nz'
      - '.dropbox.com'
      - '.pcloud.com'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of these cloud storage services.
indicators.description: Detects large outbound data transfers to cloud storage providers like Mega or Dropbox, a TTP used by ransomware groups for data exfiltration.

indicators.name: Data Staging with 7-Zip Archiving Tool
indicators.pattern:
title: Data Staging with 7-Zip Archiving Tool
id: 3c2b8d9e-f4a5-4b6c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the use of 7-Zip (7z.exe) to create a password-protected archive, a common technique used by ransomware groups to stage data for exfiltration.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQHnoEMqX-ZN1SABXBdpLEM5_A_7FZu4FuyiiBsz3x2UtkfgzccY6psCNIcRrG28JQVPw9J8lXCWwH_WLR_0oJbt1osmUS1FELlDp5qqNpRxzYBtV-47pWzlO9EyEz4-d9wtupq6QfLmdxA=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\7z.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - ' a '
      - ' -p'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of 7-Zip for creating password-protected archives by administrators or users.
indicators.description: Detects command-line usage of 7z.exe to create password-protected archives, a common data staging technique before exfiltration.

indicators.name: Rclone Execution for Data Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Rclone Execution for Data Exfiltration
id: 4d3c7e8f-a5b6-4c7d-8e1f-2a3b4c5d6e7f
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the execution of rclone.exe, a legitimate tool frequently abused by threat actors to exfiltrate data to various cloud storage backends.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQHnoEMqX-ZN1SABXBdpLEM5_A_7FZu4FuyiiBsz3x2UtkfgzccY6psCNIcRrG28JQVPw9J8lXCWwH_WLR_0oJbt1osmUS1FELlDp5qqNpRxzYBtV-47pWzlO9EyEz4-d9wtupq6QfLmdxA=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rclone.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' copy '
      - ' sync '
      - ' move '
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of rclone by system administrators for data backup or synchronization.
indicators.description: Detects the execution of rclone.exe, a tool commonly abused by threat actors to exfiltrate data to cloud storage.

indicators.name: Potential Attacker Tool Execution (rclone, 7-Zip)
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Potential Attacker Tool Execution (rclone, 7-Zip)
id: 5e4d6f7a-b6c7-4d8e-9f2a-3b4c5d6e7f8a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of rclone.exe or 7z.exe, which are legitimate tools often abused by attackers for data exfiltration and staging.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQHnoEMqX-ZN1SABXBdpLEM5_A_7FZu4FuyiiBsz3x2UtkfgzccY6psCNIcRrG28JQVPw9J8lXCWwH_WLR_0oJbt1osmUS1FELlDp5qqNpRxzYBtV-47pWzlO9EyEz4-d9wtupq6QfLmdxA=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.t1560
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative use of these tools.
indicators.description: Detects the execution of rclone.exe or 7z.exe, tools commonly used by attackers for data staging and exfiltration.

relationships.target_name: ShinyHunters Data Exfiltration and Leak Infrastructure

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Outbound Data Transfer to Cloud Storage
